perturbation.original_text: 4.7. Invoice Terms. Sekisui shall pay for each Product sold by Qualigen within 30 days after Sekisui has received the applicable invoice from Qualigen. 4.8. Marketing Claims. Sekisui covenants to Qualigen that Sekisui will not make any written or oral representation or marketing claim (either formal or informal) about any Product's capabilities or characteristics other than those representations and claims that are fully and directly supported by factual materials provided by Qualigen to Sekisui. Sekisui shall not make any false or misleading representations to customers or others regarding Qualigen or the Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.7. Invoice Terms. Sekisui shall pay for each Product sold by Qualigen within 60 days after Sekisui has received the applicable invoice from Qualigen. 4.8. Marketing Claims. Sekisui covenants to Qualigen that Sekisui will not make any written or oral representation or marketing claim (either formal or informal) about any Product's capabilities or characteristics other than those representations and claims that are fully and directly supported by factual materials provided by Qualigen to Sekisui. Sekisui shall not make any false or misleading representations to customers or others regarding Qualigen or the Products.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of Section 4.7 from '30 days' to '60 days' creates an in-text contradiction with Section 4.10, which discusses interest accrual on payments that are more than 30 days past due. This creates confusion about the actual payment deadline and when interest charges apply, impacting enforcement of payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.10. Interest. Accrual and payment of interest shall not be deemed to excuse or cure breaches of contract arising from late payment or nonpayment. Cumulative with and not exclusive of any and all other available remedies, payments that are more than 30 days past due hereunder, and which are not otherwise subject to a good faith dispute, shall accrue interest, from the due date until paid, at an annual rate equal to the prime rate, as reported in The Wall Street Journal, Eastern U.S. Edition, on the date such payment is due, plus an additional 200 basis points (2%).
perturbation.justification: The change from '30 days' to '60 days' in Section 4.7 directly contradicts Section 4.10, which states that interest accrues on payments more than 30 days past due. This inconsistency creates a direct conflict regarding when interest charges apply, impacting enforcement of payment terms and creating uncertainty about the actual payment deadline.

perturbation.original_text: 1.2. Affiliate shall mean, (i) with respect to Qualigen, any corporation or other form of business organization, which directly or indirectly owns, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with Qualigen, and (ii), with respect to Sekisui, shall mean Sekisui Diagnostics (UK) Ltd., Sekisui Diagnostics PEI, Inc., SEKISUI MEDICAL CO., LTD., and Sekisui Diagnostics GmbH. An entity shall be regarded as being in control of another entity if the former entity has the direct or indirect power to vote more than fifty percent (50%) of the outstanding voting securities (or other ownership interest for a business organization other than a corporation) of that entity, or the direct or indirect ability to direct or cause the direction of the general management and policies of the other entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. Affiliate shall mean any entity associated with Qualigen or Sekisui, including but not limited to subsidiaries, parent companies, sister companies, and strategic partners. The specific rights and obligations of affiliates under this agreement may vary and will be determined on a case-by-case basis by mutual agreement between Qualigen and Sekisui.
perturbation.explanation: The change in the definition of 'Affiliate' in Section 1.2 introduces a contradiction. The original definition specifies control through ownership of more than 50% of voting securities. The modified definition broadens the term to include 'strategic partners' and states that the 'rights and obligations' will be determined on a case-by-case basis. This contradicts sections that assume affiliates have consistent rights and obligations based on the original definition, creating uncertainty in their enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
11.1. Indemnification by Sekisui. Sekisui shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Qualigen, and its directors, officers, employees, agents and representatives (collectively, the "Qualigen Indemnitees") from and against any and all claims, suits and proceedings by a Third Party (individually and collectively, "Claims"), and any and all losses, obligations, damages, deficiencies, costs, penalties, liabilities, assessments, judgments, amounts paid in settlement, fines and expenses (including court costs and reasonable fees and expenses of attorneys), incurred in the investigation, defense and/or settlement of any Claims (individually and collectively, "Losses"; it being expressly understood, however, that incidental, special, indirect and consequential damages and lost profits, lost savings and interruptions of business are expressly excluded therefrom and from such defined term):
(a) arising out of the negligence or willful misconduct of Sekisui or its directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives in the performance of Sekisui's obligations under this Agreement; or
(b) arising out of or in connection with a breach or violation by Sekisui or its subdistributor of any applicable law or a material breach by Sekisui of any of its obligations under this Agreement, including any representations or warranties set forth herein; provided, however, that Sekisui shall have no liability or obligation to any Qualigen Indemnitee for any Claims or Losses to the extent that such Claims or Losses are primarily caused by a Qualigen Indemnitee's breach of applicable law, breach of this Agreement, negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Affiliate' broadens the term and introduces case-by-case determination of rights and obligations. This directly contradicts Section 11.1, which assumes consistent obligations for all Sekisui affiliates regarding indemnification. The original definition provided a clear standard based on control, but the revised definition creates uncertainty about whether a 'strategic partner,' now considered an affiliate, is subject to the same indemnification obligations. This ambiguity makes the indemnification clause potentially unenforceable for certain entities now classified as affiliates.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1. Price. The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Products shall be established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits. The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Reagent Kits Products shall be based upon a formula intended to ensure that Sekisui will receive 90% of the total Available Margin for all Products during the first 12 months of this Agreement, 70% of the total Available Margin for all Products during months 13-24 of this Agreement, and 65% of the total Available Margin for all Products thereafter. Accordingly, the prices (established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits) to be paid by Sekisui for the Products shall be fixed (subject to a later lookback true up) for each respective prospective six month period in the manner set forth in Exhibit D. The initial prices Sekisui agrees to pay for the respective Products for the first such prospective "six month period" (in this instance actually a five month period: May-September 2016) shall be fixed (subject to a later lookback true up) in the manner set forth in Exhibit D. Thereafter such prices shall be revisited and recalculated (prospectively) every six months in the manner set forth in Exhibit D (i.e., for purposes of such calculations for establishing the new prospective prices for the reagent products, the applicable Net Revenue, COGS and Available Margin shall be the Net Revenue, COGS and Available Margin for the applicable historical 6-month period as defined in Exhibit D). In addition, on a semi-annual basis, such amount shall be reviewed based on the actual Net Revenue, COGS and Available Margin for the 6 months then ended. In the event that such review results in a difference from the intended share of Available Margin between the Parties as contemplated above, the Parties shall make a true up payment between them in order to compensate for such overpayment or shortfall, all as provided in Exhibit D. Any true-up payments shall be paid by the applicable Party within 30 days of the receipt of an invoice for the agreed to true up amount. Sekisui shall set the customer selling prices in good faith and in a commercially reasonable manner.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1. Price. The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Products shall be established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Qualigen shall have the right to unilaterally adjust the price of the Products at any time, provided that Qualigen provides Sekisui with written notice of the price adjustment at least 30 days prior to the effective date of the adjustment. Sekisui shall set the customer selling prices in good faith and in a commercially reasonable manner.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause Section 4.1 specifies a detailed methodology for pricing and margin sharing, involving a formula based on Available Margin and periodic true-up payments. The modified clause gives Qualigen the right to unilaterally adjust prices with only 30 days' notice. This introduces an in-text contradiction with Exhibit D. The original clause describes the initial Transfer prices for all Products in aggregate are based on Qualigen's actual April 2015 - December 2015 COGS plus an amount estimated to represent Qualigen's 10% share of the actual April 2015 - December 2015. The inserted change creates uncertainty in the enforcement of the pricing terms and the true-up process, as the pre-established mechanism is now subject to Qualigen's unilateral price adjustments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exhibit D Transfer Price and True-Up Process April 22, 2016 Transfer Prices Initial Transfer Prices for all Products in aggregate are based on Qualigen's actual April 2015 - December 2015 COGS plus an amount estimated to represent Qualigen's 10% share of the actual April 2015 - December 2015 Available Margin as defined in Exhibit A and summarized in Table C. Going forward, transfer prices for Products other than reagent kits will be set as of each October 1 and April 1 for the prospective 6-month period based on Qualigen's standard unit cost in effect on the first day of the month prior to the date the new transfer prices are agreed upon (either September 1 and March 1) for the prospective 6-month period. Going forward, transfer prices for reagent kits will be set as of each October 1 and April 1 for the prospective 6-month period based on historical COGS for the earliest 6 months of the 9-month period ended the day before such date plus an amount that is estimated to represent Qualigen's applicable share of Available Margin with regard to the retrospective 6-month period as noted in Table A below. Table A below provides timeframes for the contract term. Both companies' fiscal years run from April 1st to March 31st.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a pricing formula based on Available Margin and periodic true-ups as detailed in Exhibit D. The changed text grants Qualigen the unilateral right to adjust prices with only 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the detailed pricing mechanism described in Exhibit D, making the original process unenforceable and creating a strong in-text contradiction. The altered clause breaks compatibility with Exhibit D by overriding the previously agreed-upon pricing structure, thereby causing conflict, uncertainty, and potential unenforceability of the initial transfer prices.
